codeReview.summary: Implementation fully covers the feature requirements with hierarchical span structure, start/end timestamps, parent-child relationships, tool duration breakdown, and agent decision point marking. 19 tests pass. Code is clean and well-structured.

issues.description: ToTraceSpan trait implementation does not detect LLM or agent-phase spans - always returns SpanKind::Tool for events with tool_invocation. This is inconsistent with TraceWaterfallBuilder::event_to_span which correctly uses is_agent_phase and is_llm_tool. However, the trait is a convenience extension and the main API (TraceWaterfallBuilder) handles this correctly.

input_bytes and output_bytes are always None with comment 'Would need to be tracked separately'. This is a known limitation acknowledged in the code itself. The ObservableEvent does not track input/output sizes, so this cannot be resolved without changes to the event schema.

sharedStateObservations.evidence: No external services required for this feature - pure unit tests with mock data

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation correctly integrates with the existing events module (ObservableEvent, Outcome, SpanId, TraceId). No gaps in understanding the codebase architecture.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Uses existing types from crate::events and builds upon them correctly

summary: Feature implementation passes scrutiny. All expected behaviors are implemented: hierarchical span structure with parent-child relationships, start/end timestamps per span, tool duration breakdown in TraceSummary, and agent decision points marked via is_decision_point flag. 19 comprehensive tests pass. Two non-blocking issues identified: inconsistent LLM/agent-phase detection between the main builder API and the convenience ToTraceSpan trait extension, and input_bytes/output_bytes always being None (acknowledged limitation requiring ObservableEvent schema changes). Neither issue blocks the feature from fulfilling its contract.
